segments.text: assume Layla has decided she wants a free non-surgical rhinoplasty and has CR has turned our entire content machine to justify her free non-surgical rhinoplasty we'll be going through some of that process and answering your questions as we go through I hope if I enjoy find it enjoyable and I really hope Layla likes her result before starting any non-surgical rhinoplasty I will palpate the nose to see how much potential space there is within the structure it is my belief that there is wide variation between noses and we will get some people who simply just don't have the room for filler and if you change the shape of the nose you can end up compressing the capillaries and causing compression necrosis or persistent redness which ultimately leads to requiring a reversal I do worry that some injectors Place emphasis primarily on the aesthetic result get a great before and after but then a week later need to reverse the procedure because there is so much redness caused by the

the way back to the eyes I suspect in order to include this much of a vessel you would actually need to inject much more than this because a high viscosity filler does not flow easily that far back into the eye large volumes are likely to be associated with the blindness risk and using a BD syringe make sure I'm injecting very small amounts at a time as you can see I'm also compressing the likely position of these arteries as I inject thus decreasing the potential ability of filler to flow back into the area we fear most this is simple physics increase the pressure in the areas where we don't want filler to flow and it will make it less likely product will go in this direction and more likely we will end up even if we were to get a vast reclusion with it being superficial and easier to treat diagnose and prevent injury here you can see me carefully Choosing My entry point I look for the area of

so you can see me making small adjustments always less than 0.085 mils typically less than 0.05 mils while compressing the nose to prevent backflow filler of course it would be possible without doing the safety checks without aspirating without checking capability refill without examining the nose without palpating and doing completely refill test as we go through it that I could complete this procedure in two to five minutes the reality is it took much closer to 15 to 20 minutes with multiple stages this is just how I choose to approach risk I like to think that there are multiple ways of reducing risk and over a career it is easily possible to get through it without causing any significant injuries to patients if you follow every step every time foreign
